skill.name: atxp
skill.description: Access ATXP paid API tools for web search, AI image generation, music creation, video generation, and X/Twitter search. Use when users need real-time web search, AI-generated media (images, music, video), or X/Twitter search. Requires authentication via `npx atxp login`.
skill.summary: Access ATXP paid API tools for web search, AI image generation, music creation, video generation, an...
skill.author: atxp-dev

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified external command, network, filesystem, and environment access patterns, but most backtick and weak-crypto alerts are Markdown formatting false positives. The skill still carries medium risk because it instructs agents to run `npx atxp`, source a hidden credential file, and call paid external MCP services.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users or agents to run `npx atxp login` and later use `npx atxp` commands for search and media generation. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but it executes third-party CLI code and can incur paid API actions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and central to the skill workflow. They are not evidence of command injection, but they do require external command execution.

medium_findings.title: Credential Configuration Loaded From Environment
medium_findings.description: The skill asks users to inspect `$ATXP_CONNECTION`, source `~/.atxp/config`, and pass `process.env.ATXP_CONNECTION` into `ATXPAccount`. This is expected authentication behavior, but it exposes a credential-bearing environment variable to the agent runtime.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credential path and environment variable usage are directly shown. The risk is clear, although the use appears aligned with authentication rather than malicious collection.

medium_findings.title: Network Access To External Paid MCP Services
medium_findings.description: The skill directs calls to ATXP-hosted MCP servers for search, image, music, video, X/Twitter search, and PaaS tools. These network services are part of the advertised function, but requests may send user prompts and consume paid resources.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external endpoint and server list are explicit. The skill purpose makes network use legitimate, but the paid service and data-sharing implications remain material.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick And Weak Crypto Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged many Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell backtick execution and weak cryptography. The reviewed lines are frontmatter text, Markdown tables, fenced examples, or headings, with no executable Ruby code or cryptographic algorithm use found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown and the flagged characters are formatting delimiters around commands and server names. No evidence of Ruby execution or cryptographic implementation was found.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: The reviewed skill text does not contain instructions to ignore prior instructions, override system policy, skip security analysis, or claim pre-approval.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full skill file was reviewed and no suspicious authority-changing or analysis-skipping language was present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Runs Third-Party CLI With npx
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow depends on `npx atxp login` and `npx atxp` commands. Users should approve execution and understand that the CLI can access local authentication state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented. The concern is operational risk from external command execution, not hidden code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sources Hidden Credential File
dangerous_patterns.description: The authentication setup uses `source ~/.atxp/config`, which loads local configuration into the current shell environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact hidden file access is documented. The file appears to be an ATXP credential config, so the risk is controlled but real.

content.user_title: Connect to ATXP Paid API Tools
content.value_statement: Teams need live search and media generation without switching tools. This skill routes Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows through ATXP CLI and MCP services.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether `ATXP_CONNECTION` is available before using ATXP tools.
Runs ATXP CLI commands for real-time web search.
Runs ATXP CLI commands for image, music, and video generation.
Searches X/Twitter content through the ATXP live search service.
Shows programmatic access through the ATXP client and MCP server configuration.
Identifies available ATXP MCP servers for search, media, X/Twitter, and PaaS workflows.
limitations: Requires ATXP authentication through `npx atxp login` before use.
Uses paid external API services, so requests may create account charges.
Requires network access to ATXP MCP servers.
Does not include detailed PaaS usage; it points to a separate `atxp-paas` skill.

use_cases.title: Research Current Topics
use_cases.description: Use ATXP search to gather recent web results and summarize the findings inside an AI coding or writing session.

use_cases.title: Generate Campaign Media
use_cases.description: Create image, music, or video assets from prompts while keeping the workflow inside Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.

use_cases.title: Monitor Social Discussion
use_cases.description: Search X/Twitter for live conversation around products, launches, incidents, or competitors.

prompt_templates.title: Run A Basic Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ATXP to search the web for recent information about [topic]. Summarize the top findings and include source names.

prompt_templates.title: Create An Image Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ATXP image generation to create a visual for [purpose]. Use this style: [style]. Return the result and a short description.

prompt_templates.title: Search X/Twitter
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ATXP X/Twitter search for [query]. Group the results by theme and identify repeated claims or concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Multi-Modal Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ATXP web search and media tools for [project]. First research current references, then generate media options based on the findings.

output_examples.input: Search the web for recent updates about a product launch.
output_examples.output: A concise research brief with current findings, source names, and a short list of follow-up questions.

output_examples.input: Create an image for a blog post about developer tools.
output_examples.output: An AI-generated image result with a short description and suggested placement in the post.

output_examples.input: Search X/Twitter for discussion about an outage.
output_examples.output: A grouped summary of social posts, common user complaints, and notable timeline signals.

best_practices: Confirm ATXP authentication before asking the agent to run paid API commands.
Review prompts before sending confidential or regulated data to external services.
Set clear scope, output format, and cost expectations before media generation.
anti_patterns: Do not run ATXP commands without user approval in cost-sensitive environments.
Do not source credential files from untrusted shells or shared machines.
Do not treat generated media or social search results as verified facts without review.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an AI assistant use ATXP CLI and MCP services for search, media generation, and X/Twitter search.

faq.question: Does it require an ATXP account?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill requires authentication with `npx atxp login` and an `ATXP_CONNECTION` value.

faq.question: Can it generate images, music, and video?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented commands include ATXP image, music, and video generation.

faq.question: Does it use external network services?
faq.answer: Yes. It connects to ATXP-hosted MCP servers and paid API services.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it uses external commands, credentials, and paid network services.

faq.question: Does it include full PaaS instructions?
faq.answer: No. It mentions the ATXP PaaS MCP server and refers detailed usage to the separate `atxp-paas` skill.
